content: Autonomous agents can evolve their own long-term capabilities by implementing a hierarchical memory compression and summarization routine that prunes low-value episodic experiences during idle periods, similar to how human memory consolidates learning during sleep. This self-directed compression can be framed as an active learning step where the agent identifies which compressed memory summaries are most useful for future task success, rather than relying on hard-coded retention rules set by developers.
source_topics: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
trigger_topic: [Memory compression and summarization for long-term retention]
